skill.name: compress
skill.description: Compress natural language memory files, such as CLAUDE.md, todos, and preference notes, into a shorter caveman style. The skill preserves code blocks, inline code, URLs, headings, paths, and commands while saving the original as a backup file.
skill.summary: Compress markdown and text memory files while preserving technical content.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many sensitive-path, command, and environment patterns. Review shows most sensitive-path hits are false positives because they are denylist checks, but the skill intentionally sends selected file contents to Claude or Anthropic and overwrites the original file after backup. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection text, credential harvesting, or command injection.

medium_findings.title: User File Contents Sent To External LLM
medium_findings.description: The skill builds a prompt from the target file and sends it to Anthropic when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set. It falls back to the Claude CLI, so users must treat compressed files as shared with an external model boundary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly creates a Claude message from the file-derived prompt and also supports a Claude CLI fallback. The sensitive-path denylist reduces exposure but does not remove the external data transfer risk.

medium_findings.title: In-Place File Overwrite After Compression
medium_findings.description: The skill reads the selected file, writes a backup with an .original.md suffix, and overwrites the original path with model output. This is intended behavior but can cause data loss if the backup is removed or the model output is wrong.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write operations and backup path are explicit, and validation failure restores the original. Risk remains because successful validation still replaces user content with LLM output.

low_findings.title: Sensitive Path Matches Are Denylist Guards
low_findings.description: Static findings for .env, .netrc, SSH files, AWS folders, keys, and secrets are false positives. The code checks those names and path components to refuse compression before reading or sending likely sensitive files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sensitive names appear in regular expressions and component sets used by is_sensitive_path. The function raises before file contents are read for compression.

low_findings.title: Claude CLI Subprocess Uses Fixed Arguments
low_findings.description: The subprocess finding is a legitimate external command fallback. The command is a fixed argument list without shell=True, and file content is passed through stdin as prompt text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call executes a hardcoded claude binary with a hardcoded --print argument. I did not find shell interpolation or user-controlled command arguments.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal And Reconnaissance Alerts Are Local Heuristics
low_findings.description: The path traversal and reconnaissance alerts mostly come from path examples, path regexes, file classification, and benchmark output. I did not find evidence of unauthorized directory traversal or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged code resolves a user-supplied local file and classifies or displays local data. The skill intentionally accepts a file path, so this requires user consent but is not hidden traversal.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Regex And Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Static weak-cryptography findings are false positives. The matching text appears in descriptions and regular expressions, not in cryptographic hashing, signing, encryption, or random number generation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: I did not find cryptographic operations in these locations. The reported lines are package documentation, a Claude fallback comment, and skill frontmatter.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Model Invocation With Full File Prompt
dangerous_patterns.description: The selected file content is embedded in a prompt and sent through the Anthropic SDK or Claude CLI. This is a user-visible capability, but it is a sensitive data boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code builds the compression prompt with the original text and passes it to Claude. The behavior is intentional and documented.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Rewrite Of User-Selected File
dangerous_patterns.description: The selected file is backed up and then overwritten with compressed output. This is expected for the skill, but it is a meaningful filesystem side effect.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The read, backup write, and original path write are explicit. The code includes safeguards, but the pattern remains important for marketplace disclosure.

content.value_statement: Long memory files consume context and make repeated AI sessions more expensive. This skill compresses natural language notes while preserving technical details, structure, and backup access.
actual_capabilities: Compresses markdown, text, and extensionless natural language files.
Preserves fenced code blocks, inline code, URLs, headings, paths, and commands.
Creates a human-readable backup beside the original file.
Skips common code and configuration file extensions.
Validates compressed output for preserved headings, URLs, paths, bullets, and code blocks.
Retries targeted Claude fixes when validation finds recoverable errors.
limitations: Sends selected file contents to Anthropic or the Claude CLI.
Overwrites the original file after creating a backup.
Only supports natural language files under the built-in size limit.
Compression quality depends on model output and validation coverage.

use_cases.title: Shrink Project Memory
use_cases.description: Compress a large CLAUDE.md file while keeping commands, paths, and project rules intact.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude Code or Codex

use_cases.title: Reduce Team Note Tokens
use_cases.description: Convert long onboarding notes into shorter text without losing technical references.

use_cases.title: Maintain Personal Preferences
use_cases.description: Shorten personal AI preferences and todo notes for repeated use across sessions.
use_cases.target_user: Power users managing AI memory files

prompt_templates.title: Compress One Memory File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use on a known markdown file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use compress on my CLAUDE.md file and tell me where the backup was saved.

prompt_templates.title: Compress A Todo Note
prompt_templates.scenario: Shorten a plain text planning file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress my project todo text file while preserving all commands, URLs, and headings.

prompt_templates.title: Review Then Compress
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether a file is suitable before modifying it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if this memory file is safe to compress, then run compression only if it is natural language.

prompt_templates.title: Compress With Validation Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for sensitive project notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compress this markdown memory file, validate preserved technical content, and summarize any warnings from validation.

output_examples.input: A long CLAUDE.md file with setup instructions, command examples, and project conventions.
output_examples.output: A shorter markdown memory file with the same headings, commands, URLs, and code examples preserved.

output_examples.input: A verbose team preference note that repeats the same instruction in several sections.
output_examples.output: A compact preference note that removes filler and merges repeated guidance while keeping important names and dates.

output_examples.input: A prose-heavy todo file that includes links and terminal commands.
output_examples.output: A compressed todo file with concise action statements and unchanged technical references.

best_practices: Review the file for private data before compression.
Keep the .original.md backup until you verify the compressed file.
Use this skill for prose memory files, not source code or configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not compress files that contain secrets, credentials, or private keys.
Do not use it on files where exact prose wording is legally important.
Do not remove the backup before checking important headings and examples.

faq.question: What file types does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets markdown, text, and extensionless natural language memory files.

faq.question: Does it preserve code blocks?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts and validator are designed to preserve fenced code blocks exactly.

faq.question: Does it send file contents outside my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends selected file contents to Anthropic or through the Claude CLI.

faq.question: Will it overwrite my original file?
faq.answer: Yes. It writes compressed output to the original path after saving a .original.md backup.

faq.question: Can it compress source code?
faq.answer: No. It skips common code and configuration extensions and is intended for prose.

faq.question: What happens if validation fails?
faq.answer: It retries targeted fixes and restores the original file if the final validation still fails.
